title: You don’t need a work permit - Canada.ca
content:
## You don’t need a work permit a FIFA-invited worker doesn’t need a work permit
You don’t need a work permit if you’ve been directly invited by FIFA to work or volunteer at the FIFA World Cup 26™. You’ll know you’ve been invited if you’ve received information directly from FIFA.
You may need a visitor visa or an electronic travel authorization (eTA) to come to Canada.
Apply early to avoid delays.
Find out if you need a visa or an eTA

## Flag that you’re coming for FIFA in your application
Follow the instructions in your invitation letter to let us know you’ve been invited by FIFA.
Haven’t been invited directly by FIFA?
If you haven’t been invited by FIFA to work at the event, you may need a work permit, depending on your situation.
Start over to find out what you need for the work you’ll be doing.
